memoryArtifacts.headline: Hugh Bigod Jr. --Justiciar According to Wiki
memoryArtifacts.description:
Hugh Bigod (c. 1211 – 1266) was Justiciar of England from 1258 to 1260.[1] He was a younger son of Hugh Bigod, 3rd Earl of Norfolk.

In 1258 the Provisions of Oxford established a baronial government …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh Wake II於1272年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯時，他的父親Baldwin Le Wak or Wake36歲，母親Hawise Quincy22歲。他和Joan Belauney至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他於1258年被列為chief justiciar of england。他去世於1315年5月4日在英國維爾爾阿姆斯波利享年43歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Wake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: occupational name for ‘(man of) the watch’, from Middle English wak, wake ‘wake, vigil, watch, period of guard duty’ (Old English wacu, Old Norse vaka). It alternates with the synonymous Middle English wache (Old English wacce), hence the variant Watch. The surname was also interchangeable with Waite (the equivalent Old French term) which, like Wake, was sometimes translated as Latin vigil ‘(one who is) watchful, alert’. Compare the synonymous Vail . When the bearers had high social status, the surname probably denoted an officer with responsibility for the security of a castle, town, or royal person. Compare Wakeman .

variants.name: Vail
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French de la veille ‘of the watch, watchman’, with loss of de; compare Wake . There has been some confusion with the topographic name Vale and the nickname Veal .
Scottish: shortened and altered form of McPhail , Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phàil ‘son of Paul’.

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite ‘watchman, member of the watch’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh Wake II was born in 1272, in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Baldwin Le Wak or Wake, was 36 and his mother, Hawise Quincy, was 22. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Joan Belauney. In 1258, his occupation is listed as chief justiciar of england. He died on 4 May 1315, in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 43, and was buried in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Joan Belauney. En 1258, sa profession de son indiquée est chief justiciar of england. Il est décédé le 4 mai 1315, à Amesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Wake IIが1272年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリスウオルスで生まれた時，父Baldwin Le Wak or Wakeは36歳，母Hawise Quincyは22歳であった。彼はJoan Belauneyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。1258年，職業はchief justiciar of englandと記録されている。彼は1315年5月4日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアエイメスブリーで43歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリスウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Wake II(이)가 1272년 영국 노르섬프토실르 블리스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baldwin Le Wak or Wake(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Hawise Quincy(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Joan Belauney(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1258년, 그의 직업은 chief justiciar of england이었다. 그는 1315년 5월 4일 영국 윌츠히르 아메스버이에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 블리스워스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh Wake II родился в 1272 г. в местности блисворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Baldwin Le Wak or Wake было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Hawise Quincy, 22. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Joan Belauney. В 1258 году он работал по профессии chief justiciar of england. Он умер 4 мая 1315 г. в местности амесбури, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности блисворт, нортамптоншир, Англия.
